customStyles.continueClass: mt-4 text-right text-[11px] uppercase tracking-[0.22em] text-slate-400
mt-4 text-right text-[11px] uppercase tracking-[0.22em] text-white/45

preQuestion.speaker: System
preQuestion.text:
RIFT DETECTED
TEMPORAL LOOP IDENTIFIED
STABILITY: CRITICAL

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: Ah. There you are.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: You're wondering if you've finally lost your mind. Hearing voices coming from inside your head.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: I assure you — if you had, I would summon someone else. Don't question it too much.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: Oh, how rude of me. I haven't introduced myself.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: I'm Loki, God of Stories. Though I do go by the name God of Chaos too.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: Now, you must be wondering why I might have summoned you here.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: You see, something is interfering with the natural flow of time. Not breaking it… no, that would be crude.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: I can see it. I can trace it. But I cannot reach it.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: You see, that something isn't allowing me to make any significant changes to fix the timeline.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: Which brings me to you.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: No temporal signature worth noting. No interference pattern. You're invisible to it.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: Congratulations. You are insignificant.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: For once, that's useful.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: I need someone who can move without being corrected. Someone who won't trigger whatever is rewriting things behind the scenes.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: That someone… appears to be you.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: But you'll need help. Not power. Power is loud. You need direction. Structure. Leadership.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: Find Captain America.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: Yes, I know. Of all people.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: He'll understand enough to take you seriously.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: Do try to survive. It would be terribly inconvenient if my only advantage disappeared.

customStyles.continueClass: mt-4 text-right text-[11px] uppercase tracking-[0.22em] text-slate-400
mt-4 text-right text-[11px] uppercase tracking-[0.22em] text-white/45

questionBox.titleClass: mt-3 max-w-2xl text-xl sm:text-2xl font-bold leading-relaxed text-white
questionBox.optionClass: rounded-[1.5rem] border px-4 py-4 text-left text-sm transition

preQuestion.speaker: Narrator
preQuestion.text: You follow Loki's directions and go into a city. It seems to be under attack, but not by an army. Distorted creatures flicker in and out of reality. People run… but some of them glitch. Repeating the same movement twice.

preQuestion.speaker: Captain America
preQuestion.text: Civilian! Get back — this area isn't safe!

preQuestion.speaker: Captain America
preQuestion.text: ...Wait. You're not running. And you're not glitching either. How?

preQuestion.speaker: Captain America
preQuestion.text: You're going to have to explain yourself fast. I've got about thirty seconds before those things reset.

preQuestion.speaker: Captain America
preQuestion.text: (Player explains — Loki sent them)

preQuestion.speaker: Captain America
preQuestion.text: Loki. Of course it's Loki.

preQuestion.speaker: Captain America
preQuestion.text: ...Alright. I've learned not to dismiss things just because they sound impossible. Not after everything.

preQuestion.speaker: Captain America
preQuestion.text: This whole city has been stuck in a seventeen-minute loop. Every time the loop resets, the creatures get a little more solid. A little more real.

preQuestion.speaker: Captain America
preQuestion.text: And now they're bringing something else through with them.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: Antimatter contamination. Delightful. Reality is fraying badly enough that opposing states of matter are beginning to overlap.

preQuestion.speaker: Loki
preQuestion.text: If one of those fractures collapses completely, this city ceases to be a city and becomes a cautionary tale.

preQuestion.speaker: Captain America
preQuestion.text: We've isolated three unstable sectors, but I don't know which one's about to blow first.

preQuestion.speaker: Captain America
preQuestion.text: Help me identify the right sector before containment fails.

duringQuestion.speaker: Captain America
duringQuestion.text: Captain America has isolated three unstable sectors. Identify which sector is on the verge of antimatter collapse.

duringQuestion.speaker: Captain America
duringQuestion.text: Distortion waves are destabilizing the city grid. Captain America's vibranium shield can act as a counter-resonance conductor if phase opposition is maintained.

duringQuestion.speaker: Narrator
duringQuestion.text: You reach the park. The air feels wrong here — too still. Like the city is holding its breath.

duringQuestion.speaker: Captain America
duringQuestion.text: This is the place. Every reset passes through this fountain somehow.

duringQuestion.speaker: Captain America
duringQuestion.text: At first I thought the loop repeated every cycle. It doesn't.

duringQuestion.speaker: Captain America
duringQuestion.text: The pattern resets every four loops. Three stable. One corrupted. Then it starts over again.

duringQuestion.speaker: Loki
duringQuestion.text: Ah. A recursive fracture hidden inside a larger recursion. Subtle. I approve.

duringQuestion.speaker: Captain America
duringQuestion.text: The problem is the corruption changes fast. Too fast for me to track during combat.

duringQuestion.speaker: Captain America
duringQuestion.text: But if we identify the infected loop, we can find the fracture point feeding the reset.

duringQuestion.speaker: Loki
duringQuestion.text: Find the inconsistency. The timeline will do the rest.

duringQuestion.speaker: Captain America
duringQuestion.text: Because the loop's still holding together.

duringQuestion.speaker: Loki
duringQuestion.text: Mm. Either you've found the fracture... or the timeline wants you to think you have.

duringQuestion.speaker: Loki
duringQuestion.text: Reality rarely repeats perfectly. That's the problem with forcing perfection.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: These things aren't just trapped in the loop anymore. They're interfering with the city's magnetic field.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: Every streetlight, every power relay — the whole grid's starting to destabilize.

afterAnswer.speaker: Loki
afterAnswer.text: The fractures are resonating through the infrastructure. Crude, but effective.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: Banner once told me vibranium absorbs and redistributes energy differently than normal metals.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: If he's right, my shield might be able to suppress the distortion field — but only if the resonance stays phase-opposed.

afterAnswer.speaker: Loki
afterAnswer.text: You understood approximately three more words of that sentence than I expected.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: I understood enough. I just need the correct synchronization pattern before this whole block collapses.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: That's it. That actually worked.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: ...You did good. Really.

afterAnswer.speaker: Loki
afterAnswer.text: Yes, yes. Touching. Can we move on?

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: We need more people. Whatever's causing this is bigger than a single city.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: I want Tony and Bruce in on this. Stark will complain the whole way but he'll show up. He always does.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: And Banner — if this gets physical, we're going to need the big guy.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: I'll coordinate from here. You two — go find Stark first. Last I heard he was running field tests somewhere in the Midwest.

afterAnswer.speaker: Loki
afterAnswer.text: How delightfully manual. Off you go.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: ...You sure that's the right one?

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: Because the loop's still holding together.

afterAnswer.speaker: Loki
afterAnswer.text: Mm. Either you've found the fracture... or the timeline wants you to think you have.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: Check it again. Carefully.

afterAnswer.speaker: Loki
afterAnswer.text: Reality rarely repeats perfectly. That's the problem with forcing perfection.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: That's it. That actually worked.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: ...You did good. Really.

afterAnswer.speaker: Loki
afterAnswer.text: Yes, yes. Touching. Can we move on?

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: We need more people. Whatever's causing this is bigger than a single city.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: I want Tony and Bruce in on this. Stark will complain the whole way but he'll show up. He always does.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: And Banner — if this gets physical, we're going to need the big guy.

afterAnswer.speaker: Captain America
afterAnswer.text: I'll coordinate from here. You two — go find Stark first. Last I heard he was running field tests somewhere in the Midwest.

afterAnswer.speaker: Loki
afterAnswer.text: How delightfully manual. Off you go.
